features.summary: Broadcom reported record Q2 FY2022 revenue of $8.1 billion, up 23% year-over-year, driven by strong semiconductor growth of 29% to $6.2 billion and infrastructure software growth of 5% to $1.9 billion. The company announced a $61 billion acquisition of VMware in a 50-50 cash-stock deal, which will create one of the world's largest infrastructure technology companies with approximately $40 billion in pro forma revenue. Broadcom expects Q3 revenue of $8.4 billion, representing 24% year-over-year growth.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong semiconductor revenue growth of 31% year-over-year in Q3, driven by networking (25%+ growth) and server storage connectivity (60%+ growth). The VMware acquisition is expected to close in fiscal 2023 and contribute approximately $8.5 billion in EBITDA once fully integrated. Combined software business will target mid-single digit revenue growth with mid-60s EBITDA margins, supported by the transition from perpetual to subscription licensing and leveraging expanded go-to-market capabilities.
features.risks: The VMware acquisition faces regulatory approval requirements across multiple jurisdictions and includes a 40-day 'go shop' provision allowing VMware to seek alternative buyers. Supply chain constraints continue with extended lead times, though demand remains strong with backlog growing to over $29 billion in hardware. The company will take on $8 billion in VMware net debt plus $32 billion in new debt financing, requiring rapid deleveraging from 3.5x to under 2.5x debt-to-EBITDA within two years.
features.key_drivers: Fundamental shift from proprietary to merchant silicon solutions in networking is accelerating market share gains, particularly in switching and routing across hyperscale, enterprise, and service provider segments. Content increases in next-generation server storage products are driving substantial growth as customers prefer buying complete subsystems rather than individual chips. The VMware combination will enable serving 1,500 strategic accounts directly while supporting 300,000+ customers through enhanced channel partnerships, creating significant cross-selling opportunities across the expanded software portfolio.
